sections.paragraph:
ἐξαιρέω fut. ἐξελῶ LXX (s. B-D-F §74, 3); 2 aor. ἐξεῖλον, impv. ἔξελε. Mid.: fut. ἐξελοῦμαι; 2 aor. ἐξειλάμην (-όμην v.l.; s. αἱρέω), inf. ἐξελέσθαι Ac 7:34 (=Ex 3:8; s. B-D-F §81, 3); pf. 2 sg. ἐξῄρησαι Ezk 33:9. Pass.: 1 fut. 3 sg. ἐξαιρεθήσεται Eccl 7:26, 3 pl. ἐξαιρεθήσονται Job 5:5 cod. A ; pf. ptc. ἐξῃρημένης (Ath., R. 75, 29), inf. ἐξῃρῆσθαι (Ath., R. 77, 28) (Hom.+).

① to remove someth. from its place, take out, tear out, act., τὶ someth. an eye (Heliod. 2, 16, 1 τ. ὀφθαλμὸν ἐξεῖλε τὸν δεξιόν; Heraclid., Pol. 30; Phalaris, Ep. 147, 3; Plut., Mor. 373e) Mt 5:29; 18:9 (cp. Just. A I, 15, 1 ἔκκκοψον; on the topic Jos., Ant. 6, 71). τί τινος someth. fr. someone B 6:14.

② to deliver someone from peril or confining circumstance, set free, deliver, rescue, mid. (Aeschyl., Suppl. 924; Polyb. 1, 11, 11; LXX), τινά someone Ac 7:34; 23:27; 1 Cl 52:3 (Ps 49:15). τινὰ ἔκ τινος someone fr. someth. (Demosth. 18, 90; PPetr III, 36 [a] recto, 21 ἐξελοῦ με ἐκ τῆς ἀνάγκης; Wsd 10:1; Sir 29:12; Bar 4:18, 21 al.) Ac 7:10; 12:11 (ἐκ χειρός Ex 18:4 and oft. in OT, but also Aeschin. 3, 256; see on ἐκ 1a); Gal 1:4; 1 Cl 56:8 (Job 5:19). Abs. 1 Cl 39:9 (Job 5:4).—Ac 26:17 is also prob. to be rendered deliver, save (w. HHoltzmann, Wendt, Preuschen, Zahn, Steinmann, Eng. trs., against Overbeck, Knopf, Beyer who render ‘select, choose out [for oneself]’ as Od. 14, 232; Hdt. 3, 150; LXX) τινὰ ἔκ τινος (cp. X., An. 2, 5, 20).—M-M. TW. Spicq.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

inf. inf. = infinitive

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular
  3. pl. = plural

ptc. ptc. = participle

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

act. act. = active

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

Heraclid Heraclid , perieg., III–II b.c.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. fr. = from

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

oft. oft. = often

OT OT = Old Testament

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 344
